story: Skyscrapers, yellow taxis, and now... a catchy tune! In 1985, New York City got its very own anthem. The song 'New York, New York' became the Big Apple's official theme on February 7. title: The Genius Behind Modern Communication
story: Harry Nyquist, a brilliant mind who shaped how we talk to each other today, entered the world on February 7, 1889. Born in Sweden, this future physicist and engineer would go on to crack the code of communication theory. title: Blind Artist Paints with Vision
story: Benode Behari Mukherjee saw the world differently. Born on February 7, 1904, in Behala, British India, he became a renowned artist despite being blind. Mukherjee's most famous work, the 'Lives of Medieval Saints' fresco, showcased his unique perspective. His art proved that creativity knows no boundaries, inspiring others to look beyond limitations.

title: Scaling Soviet Heights: Yevgeniy Abalakov's Birth
story: Snowy peaks and icy winds awaited Yevgeniy Abalakov from the start. Born in Yeniseysk, Russia on February 7, 1907, this future mountaineer would conquer the Soviet Union's highest point. Abalakov became the first person to reach the summit of Communism Peak, proving that determination can take you to great heights.
activity_question: What achievement made Yevgeniy Abalakov famous?

He invented a new type of ice axe
He wrote a bestselling book on mountaineering
He founded the Soviet Mountaineering Club
activity_answer: He was the first to climb Communism Peak

title: Burt Hooton: Baseball's Curveball King
story: Burt Hooton threw his way into baseball history! Born in Greenville, Texas on February 7, 1950, Hooton became a pitching ace. He tossed a no-hitter in 1972 and helped the LA Dodgers win the World Series in 1981. Known for his wicked curveball, Hooton earned a spot in the Baseball Hall of Fame.

title: A Teenage Tsar Takes the Throne
story: Imagine ruling a vast country at just 16! That's what happened to Michail Romanov on February 7, 1613. He became Tsar of Russia, starting a royal family that would lead for three centuries. Young Michail faced a tough job, bringing peace to a troubled land. His reign marked the beginning of the famous Romanov dynasty.
